Apple Scab.name: Apple Scab
Apple Scab.description: A fungal disease of apples that causes dark, scabby spots on the leaves and fruit.
Apple Scab.treatment: Prune infected leaves and apply a suitable fungicide.
Apple Scab.medicine: Captan, Myclobutanil, or Mancozeb based fungicides.
Apple Scab.prevention: Ensure good air circulation, rake and destroy fallen leaves.

Tomato Early Blight.name: Tomato Early Blight
Tomato Early Blight.description: Caused by the fungus Alternaria solani, resulting in dark, concentric ring spots on lower leaves.
Tomato Early Blight.treatment: Remove affected leaves. Do not compost them.
Tomato Early Blight.prevention: Use drip irrigation, crop rotation, and appropriate plant spacing.

Potato Late Blight.name: Potato Late Blight
Potato Late Blight.description: A devastating disease caused by Phytophthora infestans that rapidly destroys the foliage.
Potato Late Blight.treatment: Immediate application of protective or systemic fungicide. Destroy heavily infected plants.
Potato Late Blight.prevention: Plant certified disease-free seed potatoes, destroy volunteer potatoes.

Rice Leaf Blast.name: Rice Leaf Blast
Rice Leaf Blast.description: Caused by Magnaporthe oryzae, creating diamond-shaped lesions on leaves.
Rice Leaf Blast.treatment: Maintain proper flooding and apply systemic fungicides.
Rice Leaf Blast.prevention: Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilizers and use continuous flooding.

Corn Common Rust.name: Corn Common Rust
Corn Common Rust.description: Fungal disease characterized by oval, cinnamon-brown pustules on leaves.
Corn Common Rust.treatment: Fungicide application if severe before the silking stage.

Grape Black Rot.name: Grape Black Rot
Grape Black Rot.description: Fungus causing reddish-brown circular spots on leaves and rot on fruits.
Grape Black Rot.treatment: Remove infected mummies and prune for sunlight penetration.

Citrus Canker.name: Citrus Canker
Citrus Canker.description: Bacterial disease causing raised, corky lesions on leaves, stems, and fruits.
Citrus Canker.treatment: Use copper-based sprays to reduce the infection rate.
Citrus Canker.prevention: Windbreaks, clean tools, and disease-free nursery stock.

Mango Anthracnose.name: Mango Anthracnose
Mango Anthracnose.description: Fungal infection displaying dark irregular spots on leaves and blossoms.
Mango Anthracnose.treatment: Prune dead branches to lower fungal load.
Mango Anthracnose.prevention: Post-harvest hot water treatment and proper orchard spacing.

Banana Fusarium Wilt (Panama Disease).name: Banana Fusarium Wilt (Panama Disease)
Banana Fusarium Wilt (Panama Disease).description: A lethal systemic fungal disease causing yellowing of older leaves.
Banana Fusarium Wilt (Panama Disease).treatment: No viable chemical treatment once infected. Immediate eradication.
Banana Fusarium Wilt (Panama Disease).prevention: Strict quarantine, disease-free tissue culture, clean tools.

Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl.name: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl
Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl.description: A devastating viral disease spread by whiteflies causing stunted growth.
Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl.treatment: Control whitefly populations to stop the spread.
Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl.prevention: Use insect-proof netting, weed control, and resistant varieties.

Wheat Stripe Rust.name: Wheat Stripe Rust
Wheat Stripe Rust.description: Fungal disease causing yellow streaks or blisters along leaf veins.
Wheat Stripe Rust.treatment: Foliar fungicides applied at the flag leaf stage.
Wheat Stripe Rust.prevention: Grow resistant wheat cultivars and destroy volunteer wheat.

Plant Septoria.name: Plant Septoria
Plant Septoria.description: Septoria is a fungal disease that causes numerous distinctive brown spots on leaves, ultimately leading to foliage loss and reduced plant vigor.
Plant Septoria.treatment: Remove infected leaves immediately. Apply a copper-based or chemical fungicide every 7 to 10 days until harvest.
Plant Septoria.prevention: Water at the base of the plant to keep leaves dry. Practice crop rotation and ensure proper plant spacing for air circulation.

Tomato Septoria.name: Tomato Septoria
Tomato Septoria.description: Septoria leaf spot is a very common destructive disease of tomato foliage. It causes numerous circular spots on leaves with dark margins and tan to gray centers.
Tomato Septoria.treatment: Remove affected basal foliage. Do not work among the plants when they are wet. Apply fungicidal sprays.
Tomato Septoria.prevention: Destroy tomato crop debris after harvest. Crop rotation is essential.

Mildew.name: Mildew
Mildew.description: Powdery mildew is a fungal disease that affects a wide range of plants, appearing as white powdery spots on leaves and stems.
Mildew.treatment: Remove severely infected parts. Apply organic fungicides or sulfur-based sprays.
Mildew.medicine: Potassium bicarbonate, Sulfur powder, or Neem oil.
Mildew.prevention: Ensure good air circulation, avoid overhead watering, and expose to sufficient sunlight.

Rust.name: Rust
Rust.description: A common fungal disease causing orange, yellow, or brown rust-like pustules on the undersides of leaves.
Rust.treatment: Hand-pick affected leaves and destroy them. Apply rust-specific fungicidal sprays early in the morning.
Rust.prevention: Avoid wetting the foliage. Ensure wide plant spacing to keep humidity down.

Spot.name: Leaf Spot
Spot.description: A common descriptive term applied to a number of diseases affecting the foliage of plants, caused by fungi or bacteria.
Spot.treatment: Rake up and destroy fallen leaves. Treat with appropriate broad-spectrum fungicide.
Spot.prevention: Keep foliage dry, water from below, and ensure proper spacing.

Healthy.name: Healthy
Healthy.description: No structural deformities found. The cell walls and foliage are intact. The plant is demonstrating optimal health and vigor.
Healthy.treatment: No treatment necessary. Continue with regular care.
Healthy.prevention: Maintain optimal watering, balanced nutrient supply, and monitor regularly for pests.
